HR Consultant
description:
Cerchi un’azienda che investa sul tuo percorso di crescita e il tuo sviluppo e che sappia coltivare il tuo potenziale?
  
  

Tu sei il talento, noi l'opportunità!
  
  

Chi siamo?
  
  

Randstad
  
Leading Company nel settore HR.
  
Multinazionale olandese presente in 39 paesi, con oltre 280 filiali su tutto il territorio nazionale.
  
Il nostro obiettivo è essere l’ alleato per la vita professionale delle persone in ogni fase del loro percorso di carriera.
  
  

**HR Consultant:**  

In ottica di crescita e potenziamento, Randstad è alla ricerca di un HR Consultant da inserire all'interno della nostra filiale di POMEZIA.
  
  

Nei nostri uffici troverai un ambiente di lavoro stimolante, un percorso in cui metterti alla prova e mostrare il tuo reale potenziale senza limiti alla tua crescita.
  
  

L'HR Consultant, rispondendo al responsabile di filiale, fa da punto di riferimento nel mercato del lavoro per clienti e candidati.
  
  

Di cosa ti occuperai?
  
  

* attività commerciale di acquisizione nuovi clienti: organizzerai visite su appuntamento per conoscere referenti e realtà aziendali; contatto telefonico e invio di profili ma anche fidelizzazione dei clienti già attivi nel rispetto degli obiettivi definiti insieme
* attività di ricerca e selezione dei candidati attraverso pubblicazione annunci, phone interview, colloqui e assessment.
* attività amministrative di gestione dei lavoratori somministrati, assunzioni, gestione presenze e contratti


Cosa aspetti? Accetta la sfida ed entra nel nostro grande mondo dove al centro ci sono le persone!
  
  

Diventa \#HumanDiProfessione in Randstad!
  
  

Quali caratteristiche cerchiamo?
  
  

* preferibile laurea in ambito umanistico, giuridico ed economico
* attitudine commerciale e al contatto con il pubblico
* esperienza in ambito risorse umane oppure in attività di consulenza/sales


**Si offre:** inserimento con contratto a tempo determinato o indeterminato (in base alla seniority) full time, CCNL terziario, 14 mensilità, ticket restaurant, bonus, welfare aziendale, percorso di formazione continua e piano di sviluppo individuale, percorsi ed iniziative dedicate al worklife balance ed al wellbeing (benessere della persona).
  
  

Diventa \#HumanDiProfessione in Randstad!
  
  

La ricerca è rivolta ai candidati ambosessi (L.903/77\). Ti preghiamo di leggere l'informativa sulla privacy Randstad (https://www.randstad.it/privacy/) ai sensi dell'art. 13 del Regolamento (UE) 2016/679 sulla protezione dei dati (GDPR)

* Supporto alle attività amministrative dell’area risorse umane
* Gestione delle comunicazioni interne (telefoniche ed e\-mail)

* Supporto organizzativo alle attività di segreteria HR del punto vendita.


Requisiti richiesti:


* Diploma di scuola secondaria superiore

title: Responsabile Risorse Umane
description:
Il responsabile delle Risorse Umane si occupa della selezione, formazione, valutazione del personale aziendale, con riporto diretto al Consiglio di Amministrazione.

E' responsabile della corretta applicazione del CCNL aziendale (Metalmeccanico Industria), della gestione delle carriere, della corretta gestione degli obblighi del datore di lavoro.

Collabora con i responsabili di funzione aziendali (produzione, R\&S, commerciale, AFC) per il regolare svolgimento delle attività aziendali, attraverso l'autorizzazione di ferie, permessi, gestione dei turni di lavoro ed assenze.

E' responsabile della gestione delle presenze e dell'invio al consulente del lavoro dei dati necessari alla elaborazione delle buste paga, avendo pratica dei più comuni sw di gestione del personale (Zucchetti, Team System, etc). Autorizza i pagamenti al personale.

Coordina gli obblighi aziendali in termini di sicurezza, monitorando gli adempimenti e le scadenze relative al personale impiegato.

Richiesta un'ottima padronanza del pacchetto Office, in particolare Excel, deve saper elaborare prospetti di budget e di controllo del costo del personale, statistiche di presenze e produttività oraria.

* Cellulare aziendale
* Computer aziendale

title: HR Business Partner, EU One PXT
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


\*\*This role is based fulltime in Jesi, relocation package can be offered if required\*\*  

  

\*\*Questo ruolo è basato a tempo pieno a Jesi, può essere offerto un pacchetto di trasferimento se necessario\*\*  

  

Please submit your CV in English
  

  

Creating a culture where all our people feel encouraged and supported is what makes Amazon a great place to work. As an HR Business Partner, you’ll drive a positive employee experience through your detailed grasp of HR programmes, processes, and tools, offering credible and clear HR advice to Amazon’s managers. From resolving complex HR queries with colleagues to perfecting new policies and programmes, you’ll be making a real difference in how Amazon operates.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

Own and deliver projects and drive progress towards business goals  

Engage and coordinate with key stakeholders on data collection and policy implementation  

Resolve high\-level employee issues, including grievances, appeals and complex disciplinary issues  

Coach and develop HR colleagues and stakeholders across the business  

Where relevant, co\-ordinate with work councils and unions to drive positive employee relations  

Analyse internal dashboards to create reports on key metrics for leadership at your site  

  

A day in the life  

HR Business Partners get involved in a wide range of different projects and work with a mix of diverse stakeholders, all with one goal: to make Amazon a great place to work. You’ll be based in an Amazon fulfilment centre, delivery station or sortation centre and will frequently use data to inform key decisions.  

  

From one day to the next, you could be taking the lead on a range of different projects or supporting senior stakeholders with developing strategies and carrying out regular talent reviews. You’ll be taking a fresh look at our working culture and thinking of new ways to optimise them.  

  

You’ll also be supporting new colleagues to develop and learn new skills, so as well as shaping our working culture, you’ll be helping our colleagues grow. There will be plenty of opportunities for you to progress and shape your career at Amazon.  

  

About the team  

At Amazon, our HR department plays a vital role in looking after our people. Known as the People Experience and Technology Solutions (PXT) team, our priority is making the Amazon experience brilliant for our people, helping us remain one of the world’s most innovative, customer\-centric and nimble businesses.  

  

We operate proactively and take a holistic approach to people management. Our team is ‘Customer Obsessed’ and focuses on solving employee engagement issues quickly, ensuring nothing impacts our seamless employee and customer experience. Ultimately, it’s our job to insist on the highest standards and drive progress across Amazon’s strategy and goals.

**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* A degree Relevant experience in an HR role operating in an international, fast\-paced and customer\-driven environment
* Relevant experience in using data or anecdotal evidence to influence business decisions
* Relevant experience of communicating with a wide range of stakeholders, including your peers and leadership
* Proficiency in English language
* A degree Relevant experience in an HR role operating in an international

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Relevant experience working with the MS Office suite (Word, Excel, Outlook) in a professional environment

  

Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.  

  

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: HR Recruiter Specialist(protected category under Law 68/99).
Global Leaf & Product Development Trainee Program
description:
**Be a part of a revolutionary change.**  

At PMI, we’ve chosen to do something incredible. We’re totally transforming our business and building our future on one clear purpose – to deliver a smoke\-free future.  

With huge change comes huge opportunity. So, wherever you join us, you’ll enjoy the freedom to dream up and deliver better, brighter solutions and the space to move your career forward in endlessly different directions.  

Wherever you join our crucial 47,000\-strong Operations team, you’ll have the opportunity to make an impact. PMI’s shift from selling cigarettes to our new smoke\-free products has changed the way we work. New products bring new challenges – in the way we source and buy raw materials, in the way we engineer and manufacture, and in our supply chain planning.  

Bring us your curiosity and a desire to challenge the everyday, and we’ll give you lots of responsibility. You’ll help us improve, inject innovation, and create new ways of working.  

We are now recruiting for our **Global Leaf \& Product Development Trainee Program 2026**! **About the Program \-**  

Are you ready to kickstart a truly global career at the intersection of science, agriculture, innovation, and product development?  

Our Leaf \& Product Development Traineeship Program is designed for ambitious, curious early\-career professionals. Over the course of this international traineeship, you will rotate across FIVE global locations, gain hands\-on experience in key technical and operational areas, and build a strong foundation for a long\-term career within our organization**. Starting in Brazil, September 2026 and taking in 4 more international rotations through to November 2027 in Switzerland, Poland, Indonesia and Turkey.**  

This program is your opportunity to learn fast, grow globally, and make a real impact from day one!  

**What You’ll Experience \-**  


* Rotate across 5 international locations, gaining exposure to different cultures, markets, and ways of working
* Work across Leaf, Agronomy, Sustainability, and Product Development topics
* Participate in structured rotations with real responsibilities, not just observation
* Collaborate with cross\-functional and multicultural teams
* Build technical, analytical, and business capabilities through on\-the\-job learning and formal training
* Work on real business challenges, preparing cases and solutions that matter

  

Throughout the program, you will receive coaching, mentoring, and continuous feedback, helping you grow both professionally and personally.  

**Your Career Path After the Traineeship \-**  

This is not a short\-term experience.  

Successful trainees will have the opportunity to transition into permanent roles within Leaf or Product Development functions, based on business needs, performance, and individual aspirations.  

The program is built to:* Create a strong pipeline of future experts and leaders
* Enable long\-term career development across functions and geographies
* Give you visibility and credibility early in your career

  

Many of our former trainees are now key contributors within the organization.  

**Who We’re Looking For –**  

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds who bring leadership and a growth mindset. We are looking for people who are:
* Recently graduated or with up to 2–3 years of experience
* ✈️ Globally mobile and excited by international assignments
* Curious, analytical, and eager to learn
* Adaptable and comfortable in fast\-changing environments
* Strong communicators who enjoy teamwork across cultures
* Motivated to build a long\-term career, not just complete a program
* ️ A strong level of English (international working environment).

  

A background in engineering, agriculture, science, technology, or related fields is a plus, but mindset and learning agility matter most. **Expected Process Stages and Timings –**  

* Applications – 13th April\-26th April
* Virtual Interview (in English) \+ Online Cognitive \& Personality Assessment – 20th April\-10th May
* Virtual interview with the Recruiter – 27th April\-17th May
* In\-person business case Interview – Week beginning 18th May
* Offer \& Admissions process – Starting end of May
* Program starts in September 2026\.

 **Notes:**  

* Please apply to this role using CVs / Resumes in English
* All communication regarding the process will be done via email so please pay attention!
* If you have not received contact by 10th May, then you have unfortunately not been selected
* Candidates who do not complete any stage of the selection process within the specified timeframe will be disqualified.

 **Would you like to join our team and create this story together?**

title: System Administration specialist full remote
description:
Siamo un'azienda italiana operante nella ricerca e selezione del personale che si occupa di soddisfare le necessità dei clienti offrendo servizi personalizzati.

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

Siamo alla ricerca di un/una **SysAdmin** da inserire nel nostro team Infrastructure. La figura ideale è una persona con una solida base su tecnologie e Sistemi Operativi Windows e Linux, ma soprattutto con una grandissima **curiosità e voglia di imparare**. Sarà partecipe della progettazione, implementazione e manutenzione dei sistemi operativi dei clienti , garantendone scalabilità, affidabilità e sicurezza.


Non ci aspettiamo che tu sappia già tutto. Ciò che per noi conta di più è la tua **proattività** nel proporre soluzioni, la tua capacità di **lavorare efficacemente in team** e la tua dedizione alla crescita professionale e personale.

#### **Le tue responsabilità**

* Gestire l’intero ciclo di vita della nostra infrastruttura server (fisica e virtuale), inclusa l’installazione, la configurazione, la manutenzione e il troubleshooting.
* Collaborare con i team di sviluppo e con i clienti per garantire che le applicazioni abbiano un’infrastruttura solida su cui operare.
* Garantire la stabilità, la sicurezza, le performance e l’alta affidabilità dell’intera infrastruttura IT.
* Partecipare attivamente alla vita del team, condividendo conoscenze e contribuendo a un ambiente di lavoro positivo e collaborativo.
* Ricercare e proporre nuovi strumenti e tecnologie per migliorare continuamente l’efficienza, l’automazione e la resilienza della nostra infrastruttura.

**Requisiti:**
--------------

#### **Competenze Minime Necessarie**


Per avere successo in questo ruolo, è fondamentale avere una comprensione pratica dei seguenti concetti e strumenti. Il tuo entusiasmo nell’approfondirli è altrettanto importante!

* Sistemi Operativi: Gestione approfondita di sistemi Linux (in particolare distribuzioni come Debian/Ubuntu o RHEL/CentOS/Rocky). La conoscenza di Windows Server aiuta nella gestione dell’infrastrutture.
* Virtualizzazione: Esperienza pratica con le principali piattaforme di virtualizzazione, come VMware vSphere o Proxmox VE.
* Servizi di Base: Capacità di installare, configurare e manutenere servizi essenziali come web server (NGINX, Apache), database (MySQL/MariaDB, PostgreSQL) e server DNS.
* Networking: Solida conoscenza dei concetti di rete (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, routing, firewalling).
* Scripting e Automazione: Capacità di scrivere script (principalmente in Bash, ma anche Python o PowerShell) per automatizzare compiti ripetitivi e gestire le configurazioni.
* Controllo di Versione: Esperienza con Git per la gestione di script e file di configurazione.

#### **Costituiscono un plus (ma non sono indispensabili)**

* Conoscenza di provider cloud (AWS, GCP, Azure) e dei loro servizi IaaS (EC2, VPC, S3\).
* Esperienza con strumenti di Infrastructure as Code (IaC) e Configuration Management come Ansible o Terraform.
* Familiarità con strumenti di monitoraggio e observability (Prometheus, Grafana, Zabbix, ELK Stack).
* Conoscenza di tecnologie di containerizzazione (Docker) e una comprensione di base di orchestratori come Kubernetes.
* Esperienza nella progettazione e gestione di strategie di backup e disaster recovery (es. Veeam).

#### **Il/La candidato/a ideale**

* Ha una mentalità proattiva: non aspetta che gli vengano assegnati i compiti, ma identifica problemi e propone soluzioni.
* È un giocatore di squadra: ama collaborare, condividere idee e aiutare i colleghi.
* Possiede una forte passione per l’apprendimento: vede ogni sfida come un’opportunità per acquisire nuove competenze.
* Ha buone capacità comunicative ed è in grado di spiegare concetti tecnici complessi in modo

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

**Cosa si offre:**

* Un ambiente di lavoro flessibile e di supporto.
* Opportunità di formazione e sviluppo professionale (certificazioni, conferenze).
* Un pacchetto retributivo competitivo.
* La possibilità di lavorare con tecnologie all’avanguardia e di avere un impatto reale sulla stabilità e la crescita della nostra azienda.

title: Responsabile Risorse Umane, Servizi Gestione Esigenze Domestiche
Senior Motor Fleet Underwriter — Europe
description:
**Who We Are**
--------------



Wakam is a **B2B2C insurance company** that creates **white\-label insurance solutions** via its **Play\&Plug®** technology platform for more than **80 partners**. We provide most of our insurance products through **APIs** and operate across **32 countries**, making Wakam the **European leader in digital and embedded insurance**. Wakam is also a **mission\-driven company** dedicated to **"enabling transparent and impactful insurance."**



**About the Team**
------------------



You will join Wakam's **Motor Underwriting team**, which plays a central role in shaping underwriting performance, portfolio quality, and sustainable growth across multiple European markets.



Working at the crossroads of **Underwriting, Pricing, Claims, Distribution, Product, Reinsurance, and Compliance**, the team supports Wakam's broker, MGA, and platform partnerships with strong technical underwriting expertise and a data\-driven approach to decision\-making.



**Your Mission**
----------------



As a **Senior Motor Fleet Underwriter (12 months fixed\-term)**, you will take ownership of Wakam's **European commercial motor fleet portfolio**, driving underwriting performance, strengthening broker and MGA relationships, and contributing to product and market strategy across several jurisdictions.



You will act as a **technical authority** on fleet underwriting, combining risk selection, pricing discipline, market insight, and portfolio steering to ensure profitable and sustainable growth. This role requires both hands\-on underwriting expertise and the ability to influence underwriting frameworks, appetite, and governance.



**✏️ Your Responsibilities**
----------------------------


### **Portfolio Underwriting \& Risk Assessment**



You will underwrite **new business and renewals** across commercial motor fleet segments, including **PHV/taxi, delivery, leasing fleets, and other types of fleets**. You will assess risk quality using exposure data, loss history, fleet management practices, driver profiles, and sector information, and apply technical pricing models and rating tools to determine adequate premiums.



You will negotiate terms with brokers and MGAs to achieve balanced outcomes on **price, coverage, and conditions**, while applying underwriting authority appropriately and escalating referrals when needed. You will also manage quotes, binders, endorsements, and monitor in\-force accounts for mid\-term changes, fleet movements, and deterioration in claims performance.


### **Portfolio Management \& Performance**


* You will monitor the **combined operating ratio (COR)** and **loss ratio** of the fleet portfolio on a regular basis, identifying trends and taking corrective actions where needed. You will track performance by segment, geography, broker, vehicle type, and fleet size, and contribute to quarterly portfolio reviews and management reporting.
* You will actively manage underperforming accounts through rate action, tighter coverage terms, or non\-renewal decisions where appropriate. You will also produce underwriting MI and data analysis to support portfolio steering and work closely with actuarial teams on pricing adequacy, experience reviews, and reserving discussions.

### **Pricing \& Technical Development**


* You will contribute to the development and ongoing refinement of **fleet pricing models, rating tools, underwriting guidelines, referral matrices, and appetite statements**. You will incorporate market intelligence and claims experience into pricing assumptions, including repair cost inflation, parts trends, and frequency developments.
* You will also monitor external market conditions such as competitor pricing, capacity changes, and emerging risks including **EV fleets, ADAS technology, autonomous vehicles, gig economy operators, and mobility\-as\-a\-service platforms**.

### **Broker \& Distribution Management**


* You will build and maintain strong relationships with **specialist fleet brokers, Lloyd's brokers, and MGA partners** across Europe.
* You will lead broker meetings, scheme reviews, and renewal discussions, support the onboarding of new fleet schemes and delegated authority arrangements, and represent Wakam in market discussions and industry forums.

### **European \& Cross\-Border Underwriting**


* You will underwrite **cross\-border fleet risks** and multi\-jurisdiction programmes, navigating local regulatory requirements, mandatory covers, and market practices across countries such as the **UK, France, Spain, Italy, Germany, the Netherlands, Belgium, and the Nordics**.
* You will liaise with local contacts, and MGA's where relevant, while monitoring macroeconomic and geopolitical factors that may impact portfolio performance.

### **Claims Collaboration, Fraud \& Governance**


* You will work closely with claims teams on large or complex fleet losses, support claims reviews, identify fraud indicators and leakage patterns, and contribute to wording reviews to ensure coverage remains robust and fit for purpose.
* You will operate within underwriting authorities and governance frameworks, maintain accurate records in underwriting systems, prepare submissions for internal committees, and support audit and compliance processes across relevant markets.

**What You Bring**
------------------



You bring significant technical underwriting experience and strong commercial judgement. In particular, you have:


* **Significant motor fleet underwriting experience**, ideally within an insurer, Lloyd's syndicate, or specialist MGA
* A proven track record managing **fleet portfolios across multiple European markets**
* Experience underwriting **large and complex fleet accounts**, including structures involving deductibles, captives, or self\-insured elements
* Strong technical underwriting capabilities in **risk assessment, pricing, coverage structuring, and portfolio steering**
* Comfort interpreting **loss runs, claims statistics, and actuarial analyses**
* Strong command of tools such as **Excel**, and ideally **Power BI** or similar BI platforms, plus familiarity with underwriting and pricing systems
* Good understanding of **reinsurance structures** relevant to fleet business, such as **XL, quota share, and aggregate covers**
* Familiarity with **European motor insurance regulation**, including cross\-border requirements and compulsory insurance frameworks
* Fluent **English**; an additional European language such as **French, Spanish, German, Italian, or Dutch** would be a strong plus
* A professional qualification such as **ACII** (or equivalent), or progress toward it, is preferred.

**✨ Who You Are**
-----------------


* You are a technically strong and commercially minded underwriter who knows how to balance **discipline and growth**.
* You are confident in broker\-facing environments, credible in committee discussions, and comfortable making decisions on complex risks.
* You combine analytical rigour with sound judgement, communicate clearly with technical and non\-technical stakeholders, and are able to manage a significant portfolio with autonomy.
* You thrive in a fast\-paced, international, technology\-led environment and bring a collaborative mindset when working with Pricing, Claims, Legal, Product, and Distribution teams.

**✨ AI \& Digital Mindset**
---------------------------



At Wakam, underwriting is increasingly supported by **data, tooling, and automation**. In this role, you will be expected to work in a way that is structured, measurable, and scalable: using data to support underwriting decisions, improving monitoring practices, and contributing to more robust and efficient underwriting frameworks.



An interest in digital underwriting tools, portfolio analytics, and technology\-enabled decision\-making will help you succeed in this environment.



**Why Join Wakam?**
-------------------



Joining Wakam as a **Senior Motor Fleet Underwriter** means taking ownership of a meaningful European fleet portfolio in a business where underwriting expertise has direct visibility and impact.



You will benefit from genuine underwriting autonomy, broad market exposure, and close collaboration with expert teams across actuarial, claims, product, and distribution. It is an opportunity to shape underwriting strategy in a fast\-growing, technology\-driven insurer with a strong international footprint and real ambition in Motor.



**Key Performance Indicators**
------------------------------



Success in the role will typically be measured through indicators such as:


* Fleet portfolio **COR** versus target
* New business **GWP** written versus budget, by market and segment
* Renewal retention across managed accounts
* Pricing adequacy versus technical rate
* Quote\-to\-bind conversion
* Underwriting file quality / audit score
* Broker relationship quality and responsiveness
* Referrals handled within agreed SLA

**Hiring Process**
------------------



Our hiring process is designed to be **selective, transparent, and respectful of your time**:



* HR Interview


* **Interview with our Chief Underwriting Officer \- Motor and Mobility**


* **Interview with our SVP, Group Chief Underwriting Officer**


### **AI\-Assisted Interview Process Policy \| Notion**

**More About Us**
Check out our website to learn about the 11 cultural markers that make Wakam truly special! If you're adventurous, impact\-driven, and ready to shape the future of insurance, we'd love to meet you!

 **What Makes Us Unique:**
* True remote work flexibility with our Wakam From Anywhere (WFA) program \- yes, we even have a teammate working from a sailboat!
* Flat hierarchical system promoting direct impact and autonomy
* Monthly Free.day: dedicated time for personal growth and skills development


* Lunch voucher with Swile card


* A meaningful company: we became a Mission\-driven company in March 2021


* Work alongside passionate experts: who will share their knowledge and help you develop and grow in your career.



At Wakam, our "Free to Impact" culture is built on four essential pillars that shape everything we do :

️ **Communication \& Knowledge Management**
* Foster transparent collaboration across our flat organizational structure


* Share knowledge freely in our highly collaborative environment


* Contribute to our collective intelligence through open dialogue



 **Curiosity**
* Embrace our monthly Free.day for learning and skills sponsorship


* Think big and challenge conventional insurance norms


* Stay eager to learn and explore new possibilities



 **Continuous Improvement**
* Take initiative to transform the insurance industry


* Turn challenges into opportunities for innovation


* Constantly evolve our ways of working



 **Outcome Oriented**
* Focus on impact rather than hierarchy


* Drive results through clear objectives and autonomy


* Transform bold ideas into concrete solutions



***At Wakam, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment where diversity is celebrated. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please feel free to reach out to your recruiter.***

NORAUTO S.p.a. e' una multinazionale francese leader europea nella Grande Distribuzione Organizzata per la vendita ed installazione di pneumatici, elettronica, accessori e ricambi auto.
  
  

**ALLIEVO MANAGER VENDITA \- LAZIO:**  

Il candidato, affiancando il manager vendita senior e in accordo con le politiche aziendali, sarà inserito in un percorso formativo (della durata variabile dai 6 ai 12 mesi) con l’obiettivo di far acquisire le competenze e gli strumenti necessari a ricoprire successivamente ruoli di responsabilità
  
  

Le principali attivita' in cui verra' formato e coinvolto sono:
  
  

* gestire il Cliente,
* monitorare e supervisionare con il Direttore gli aspetti economici del reparto del centro: conto economico, spese e budget;
* gestire gli aspetti commerciali, rispettando gli standard aziendali nell'implementazione delle azioni commerciali e promozionali e i criteri di Visual Merchandising;
* supporto nel monitoraggio della rotazione ed il flusso della merce in assortimento,
* supportare il Direttore nella gestione delle risorse umane: motivazione, formazione e crescita,
* garantire il raggiungimento degli obiettivi qualitativi e quantitativi del reparto,
* supportare il Direttore nel coordinare tutte le attivita' del centro Norauto.


Profilo
  
  

**La figura ricercata deve possedere:**  

* un’esperienza di almeno 2/3 anni in ruoli analoghi nella GDO
* passione per il contatto con il pubblico
* capacità di gestione commerciale, di decisione e orientamento ai risultati
* predisposizione alla gestione dei team, unitamente ad un forte orientamento verso la crescita professionale propria e dei collaboratori


Cosa offriamo
